title: --health-start-period ignored if --health-cmd not set
body:
### Issue Description

Flag --health-start-period is ignored if --health-cmd  is not explicitly set in run command (health command from the image is used). In such case container is marked immediately after start as unhealthy and if --health-on-failure=kill is set it is destroyed.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run -d --rm --health-interval 15s --health-on-failure kill --health-start-period 10s docker.io/nodered/node-red:latest


### Describe the results you received

Container is marked immediately as "unhealthy" and killed

### Describe the results you expected

Container should be marked as "starting" for initial 15s and afterwards as "healthy". Correct behaviour can be observed with this command (health check command extracted from the image):
podman run -d --rm --health-interval 15s --health-on-failure kill --health-start-period 10s --health-cmd="curl 
http://localhost:1880/ || exit 1" docker.io/nodered/node-red:latest

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This problem should be fixed with https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25681
